steps.title: Factor the second equation to match the structure of the first
steps.content:
We are given $\frac{x}{y} = 87$ and $\frac{cx}{3y} = 87$.

Rewrite the second equation by separating the constant multiplier from the ratio:
$$\frac{cx}{3y} = \frac{c}{3} \cdot \frac{x}{y} = 87$$

steps.title: Substitute and solve for c
steps.content:
Since $\frac{x}{y} = 87$, substitute this value:
$$\frac{c}{3} \cdot 87 = 87$$

Divide both sides by 87:
$$\frac{c}{3} = 1$$

Multiply both sides by 3:
$$c = 3$$
